reason: Jurors in a homicide trial must choose between convicting with uncertain evidence or freeing a potentially guilty teen.
A mother faces the impossible decision of killing her abusive husband to protect her child.
A submarine captain must defy or obey orders to launch nuclear missiles, each choice risking countless lives.
A detective must choose to follow protocol or use lethal force to stop a calculating serial killer.
An honest family man must betray his own kin or uphold justice, risking personal ruin.
A rookie senator must decide between honest reform or political compromise to expose deep corruption.
A factory owner must risk his fortune and safety to shelter the oppressed under a brutal regime.
A violent criminal must choose between losing his free will or returning to his former brutality.
A reformed gunslinger must decide if taking up arms again is justified to save the innocent.
A warrior must choose between loyalty to his king or fighting for his adopted people’s freedom.
